rationale: My round-6 dissent (worktree-enum forks the shared predicate) is directly mitigated. Verified at 50165d81: FindOrphanedClosedBeads stays the byte-identical fail-open wrapper for complete/next/doctor; ScanOrphanedClosedBeads is an error-preserving extraction (AC1 mixed-list parity pins wrapper equivalence); the worktree-enum leg lives entirely in internal/approve and gives the shared predicate NO worktree awareness. It is ADDED, not a replacement, and is keyed off bead.WorktreeList() (bdcli.go:201) = defaultWorktreeOps.List() = the same source FinalizeEpic consumes at :383. ADR-0030 clean: approve already imports internal/bead (impl.go:13); the bd query is not executor git-I/O; no internal/bead change lands. Ownership: internal/lifecycle/** claimed by exactly one domain (workflow); AC10's named structural test is absent at 50165d81 = RED-on-revert, replacing the non-discriminating grep -l. blp6 constraint pinned to the same enumeration source (AC13). Sole caveat: the leg's gitutil.IsAncestor adds an approve->gitutil edge, but it is a read-only shared utility identical to lifecycle's existing usage, not executor I/O, and does not breach the executor boundary. No fork, no misfit, no ownership disturbance.

findings.note: Shared FindOrphanedClosedBeads predicate is UNCHANGED for complete/next/doctor; the worktree-enum leg is added in internal/approve only (spec R1, Scope line 52, Non-Goals line 72). The lifecycle refactor is an error-preserving extraction (ScanOrphanedClosedBeads core + fail-open wrapper), pinned byte-identical by AC1's mixed-list wrapper-parity subcase. My round-6 fork concern is resolved: the gate has a merge-prevention leg PLUS the unchanged shared predicate for breadth.

Verified defaultWorktreeOps.List() -> bead.WorktreeList() (mindspec_executor.go:44-46) is the SAME source FinalizeEpic's loop consumes at :383, filtered on HasPrefix(e.Branch, BeadBranchPrefix) at :385 (WorktreeListEntry.Branch field confirmed). Keying the gate off the same enumeration closes the round-6 desync between scan and merge.

internal/approve/impl.go:13 already imports internal/bead; bead.WorktreeList (bdcli.go:201-217, bd worktree list --json) is a bd-domain query, not executor git-I/O. No internal/bead change lands; consumed read-only; correctly declared not-a-touched-surface in Impacted Domains.

internal/lifecycle/** claimed only by workflow/OWNERSHIP.yaml (no other domain claims it). AC10 upgrades the pre-applied non-discriminating grep -l to a named RED-on-revert structural test TestLifecycleOwnershipExactlyOneWorkflowClaimant, confirmed ZERO hits at 50165d81.

Non-Goals line 74 constrains any future FinalizeEpic loop change to preserve worktree-enumerated-real-branches (AC12) AND stay keyed to the same WorktreeOps.List/bd-worktree-list source the R1 leg scans (AC13). Grounded and coherent; blp6 stays deferred P2, loop behaviorally untouched.

NON-BLOCKING. The worktree-enum leg's gitutil.IsAncestor check adds a new internal/approve -> internal/gitutil import edge: approve does not import gitutil today (impl.go:249 uses exec.MergeBase; Executor exposes MergeBase but not IsAncestor). Evidence-refuted as non-blocking: gitutil is a leaf shared utility, not the executor boundary; the identical read-only ancestry primitive is already called by internal/lifecycle (which approve consumes) and by FinalizeEpic itself; the spec lands NO gitutil change, so no ownership edge. The spec's ADR-0030 claim ('no executor git-I/O enters internal/approve') stays literally true. Plan/bead may prefer routing ancestry through an implXxxFn seam (default gitutil.IsAncestor) for symmetry; noted, not required.
